reasoning: The European Central Bank is engaged in active Quantitative Tightening (QT). By late 2025, both the Asset Purchase Programme (APP) and the Pandemic Emergency Purchase Programme (PEPP) will be in full run-off, meaning the ECB's balance sheet should structurally shrink by roughly 30 to 45 billion EUR per month. Therefore, any forecast looking approximately 3 months forward from October 2025 to late January 2026 expects a base-case reduction of $> 100$ billion EUR. Year-end liquidity injections via Main Refinancing Operations (MROs) typically unwind by the latter half of January. Consequently, the balance sheet increasing over this horizon would likely require a severe economic shock, sovereign debt crisis, or banking distress that forces the ECB to suddenly halt QT, trigger the Transmission Protection Instrument (TPI), or inject massive long-term liquidity. The probability of such an abrupt policy reversal within a 3-month window is relatively low (around 10-15%).
